Loadout UX Redesign: Phase 3 - Loadout Contents Split View
description:
# Phase 3: Loadout Contents Split View

## Why
The loadout detail view's Contents tab currently shows a static list of items grouped by type. Users can't preview what's in a loadout without navigating away. Adding a split view lets users evaluate loadout contents in-place.

## Scope
- Split view for Contents tab (reuses splitViewModel from Phase 2)
- Pane navigation consistent with Phase 2
- Item source display (registry name or "library")

## Dependencies
Requires Phase 2 (reuses splitViewModel component)

## Plan Reference
Full details in docs/loadout-ux-redesign.md (Phase 3, Tasks 3.1-3.2)
close_reason: All implementation tasks complete. Phase 1: action buttons + wizard screen. Phase 2: removed Overview tab, added split-view Files tab, full wizard implementation, deleted old modal. Phase 3: loadout detail split-view content browser.

title: Task 2.4: Add split-view to Files tab with reusable splitViewModel
description:
# Split-View Files Tab

## Why
With Overview tab removed, the Files tab becomes the primary view. A split layout (file tree + preview) lets users see file contents without extra navigation. The splitViewModel is designed as a reusable component for Phase 3.

## Description
Transform the Files tab into a split-view layout.

### Component Design
Build as a standalone splitViewModel:
- Left pane: file tree with cursor, scroll
- Right pane: preview content with independent scroll
- focusedPane field (0=left, 1=right)
- collapsed bool for narrow terminals
- showingPreview bool for single-pane mode

### Layout
- Left pane: 35% width (min 25 chars)
- Right pane: 65% (remaining)
- Separator: | character
- Activation: content width >= 70 (not terminal width)
- Adaptive ratio: 40/60 at content widths 70-90, 35/65 at 100+

### Single-Pane Fallback (content width < 70)
Replace-in-place interaction:
- Mode 1 (file tree): full-width tree, Enter opens file
- Mode 2 (file content): full-width content replaces tree, Esc returns to tree with cursor preserved

### Primary File by Type
Skills -> SKILL.md, Agents -> .md file, Rules -> rule .md, Hooks -> config, MCP -> config, Commands -> command file, Loadouts -> loadout.yaml

### File Tree
- Directories always expanded (no collapse toggle)
- 2 spaces indentation per nesting level
- Built from ContentItem.Files []string

### Preview
- Glamour render for markdown, plain text for others
- Render cache keyed by file path
- First 200 lines for large files with "(N more lines)" indicator
- Binary files: "(binary file)" placeholder
- Width-constrained glamour style matching pane width

## Success Criteria
- Split view at content width >= 70
- Single-pane replace-in-place at content width < 70
- Primary file pre-selected and previewed on entry
- File tree navigation updates preview in real-time
- Preview scrolls independently when focused
- Glamour cache prevents re-renders on revisit
- Works for all content types

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: split view renders at 120x40
2. Unit test: single-pane file tree at 60x20
3. Unit test: Enter opens file in single-pane, Esc returns with cursor preserved
4. Unit test: primary file selection for each content type
5. Unit test: cursor navigation updates preview content
6. Unit test: independent preview scroll
7. Unit test: glamour cache hit on revisit
8. Unit test: 200-line cap for large files
9. Golden files at all size variants
10. Boundary: 50+ files (tree scroll)

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/splitview.go (NEW — reusable component)
- cli/internal/tui/detail.go
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go
close_reason: Already completed in earlier commits (86cb395, f6b7935)

```go
version := "─"
if entry.version != "" {
    version = entry.version
}
meta := fmt.Sprintf("%s  v%s  %d items", status, helpStyle.Render(version), entry.itemCount)
```

## Options
- Show "v─" as just "─" (no "v" prefix when no version)
- Show nothing for version when absent
- Show "no version" text

title: Task 1.3: Replace flat item list with per-type item selection
description:
# Per-Type Item Selection Steps

## Why
A single flat list of all catalog items doesn't scale and doesn't help users think about what they're building. Walking through each content type individually matches the mental model and supports provider-specific filtering.

## Description
Each selected content type gets its own wizard step with a checkbox list of items.

### Entry Filtering
When the provider is selected, build a map[ContentType][]loadoutItemEntry that pre-filters all catalog items by type and provider compatibility. This map is reused across all steps. The t key toggles "show all" mode where incompatible items appear grayed out (muted + strikethrough + "(incompatible)" suffix).

### Per-Type State
Each type step preserves its own cursor position, scroll offset, and search query in maps (map[ContentType]int). Navigating back restores exact position. No state is cleared on Esc — only step and typeStepIndex change.

### Step Tracking
Composite approach: existing step enum + typeStepIndex int + selectedTypes []ContentType. When step == clStepItems, typeStepIndex indicates which type is active. Display: fixedStepsBefore + typeStepIndex + 1 of fixedSteps + len(selectedTypes).

### Empty Types
If a selected type has 0 available items, show the step with "No {type} available for {provider}" and require Enter to proceed. Do NOT auto-advance.

### Keys
- Space: toggle selection
- a: Select All / Deselect All
- t: toggle compatibility filter (show all vs compatible only)
- /: search within current type
- Enter: next type step
- Esc: previous type step (or type selection if on first type)

## Success Criteria
- Each content type gets its own step with pre-filtered items
- t toggles compatible-only vs show-all modes, current mode visible in help text
- Incompatible items use strikethrough + "(incompatible)" — not just color
- Search filters within current type, selections persist after clearing
- Back-navigation preserves selections, cursor, and scroll offset from all previous steps
- Empty types show message, require Enter
- a key selects/deselects all visible items
- Mouse: clickable checkboxes and items

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: Rules step shows 3 compatible items, Space toggles
2. Unit test: t key reveals incompatible items grayed out with strikethrough
3. Unit test: a key selects/deselects all
4. Unit test: search reduces visible items, selections persist after clearing
5. Unit test: Esc from first type -> type selection, all selections preserved
6. Unit test: Esc from Rules -> advance back to Rules -> cursor position preserved
7. Unit test: empty type step shows message, Enter advances, Esc goes back
8. Unit test: per-type scroll offset preserved on back-nav
9. Integration test: full flow through 3 types, all selections in manifest
10. Golden file: per-type item selection step (80x30)

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go
- cli/internal/catalog/types.go (ContentType)
- cli/internal/provider/provider.go (SupportsType)
close_reason: Implementation complete from prior sessions. Added 11 missing TDD tests (toggle-all, incompatible rendering, search persistence, back-nav cursor/scroll preservation, empty type handling, full flow integration) plus empty-type rendering code path.

## Why

These are the most likely source of bugs:
- Select provider -> select 3 types -> select 2 Rules items -> back to type selection -> verify 3 types still checked
- Select items in Rules -> advance to Skills -> back to Rules -> verify Rules selections intact
- Complete to review -> back to name -> change name -> advance to review -> verify new name but item selections unchanged

title: Clear status messages on keypress
description:
## Phase 3: Clear status messages on keypress

### Problem
statusMessage is write-only. Once set, it persists forever. Stale messages mislead users about current state.

### Implementation
Add at the TOP of the tea.KeyMsg case in App.Update(), BEFORE delegating to screen-specific handlers:

```go
case tea.KeyMsg:
    // Clear transient status on any keypress
    if a.statusMessage != "" {
        a.statusMessage = ""
        a.statusIsErr = false
        a.statusWarnings = nil
    }
```

This matches the existing pattern in settings.go (~line 55).

Only clear on actual keypresses (tea.KeyMsg), not on async message completions or other msg types.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — tea.KeyMsg handler in App.Update()

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Status message, statusIsErr, and statusWarnings are cleared on any keypress
- [ ] Clearing happens BEFORE screen-specific key handling (so the keypress that clears also does its normal action)
- [ ] Async message completions (registryAddDoneMsg, etc.) do NOT trigger clearing
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not add timer-based clearing (keypress-based is sufficient per plan)

title: Clone cleanup on all dismiss paths
description:
## Phase 2d: Clone cleanup on all dismiss paths

### Problem
If user dismisses the "Not a Syllago Registry" confirmation modal without confirming, the preserved clone must be cleaned up.

### Implementation
In the handler for the confirmation modal's dismiss/cancel:

1. **Cancel button clicked** -> os.RemoveAll(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
2. **Esc pressed** -> same cleanup (both handled in the modal's dismiss path)
3. **User confirms -> import flow** -> importModel takes ownership via initFromExternalClone (Phase 2c handles this)

Note: Sidebar clicks during a modal are NOT reachable (focus is trapped to focusModal), so that path doesn't need handling.

After cleanup on cancel/esc paths, clear the pending fields:
```go
os.RemoveAll(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
a.pendingNonSyllagoClone = ""
a.pendingNonSyllagoScan = catalog.NativeScanResult{}
```

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — confirmation modal cancel/dismiss handler

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Cancel removes the clone directory (os.RemoveAll)
- [ ] Esc removes the clone directory
- [ ] pendingNonSyllagoClone is cleared to "" after cleanup
- [ ] pendingNonSyllagoScan is cleared after cleanup
- [ ] Confirm path does NOT clean up (import model takes ownership)
- [ ] No leaked temp directories after any dismiss path
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify import.go (Phase 2c handles that)

description: Symlink-tracing and hash-match fallback taint propagation in writeItem(). Prevents laundering private content through provider round-trip or copy-install.

title: 5.1: Add symlink-tracing taint propagation in writeItem()
description:
File: cli/internal/add/add.go

Add traceSymlinkTaint(path string) (sourceRegistry, sourceVisibility string) helper:
1. Check if path is a symlink via os.Lstat
2. Resolve via os.Readlink + filepath.EvalSymlinks
3. Check if target is inside catalog.GlobalContentDir()
4. Walk up from target to find .syllago.yaml with SourceVisibility set
5. Return taint fields if found

Add isDir(path) bool helper.

In writeItem(), before hash computation: call traceSymlinkTaint(item.Path). If taint found and opts.SourceVisibility is empty, propagate to opts.

Commit: feat(add): symlink-tracing laundering defense in writeItem()

title: 5.2: Write tests for symlink-tracing taint propagation
description:
File: cli/internal/add/add_test.go

3 tests:
- TestTraceSymlinkTaint_DirectSymlink: create fake library item with private taint, create symlink to it, assert traceSymlinkTaint returns private/acme-internal. Uses catalog.GlobalContentDirOverride.
- TestTraceSymlinkTaint_NonSymlink: regular file -> returns empty
- TestTraceSymlinkTaint_SymlinkOutsideLibrary: symlink to dir outside GlobalContentDir -> returns empty

Commit: test(add): symlink-tracing taint propagation tests

title: 5.3: Add hash-match fallback taint propagation in writeItem()
description:
File: cli/internal/add/add.go

Add hashMatchTaint(contentHash, globalDir string) (sourceRegistry, sourceVisibility string) helper:
1. Walk globalDir looking for .syllago.yaml files
2. For each, load metadata
3. Skip items without private taint
4. If SourceHash matches contentHash, return taint fields
5. Use filepath.SkipAll to stop on first match

In writeItem(), after symlink check (Task 5.1): if opts.SourceVisibility still empty, call hashMatchTaint(hash, globalDir). Propagate if found.

Import: registry package (for IsPrivate).

Commit: feat(add): hash-match fallback laundering defense in writeItem()

title: 5.4: Write tests for hash-match taint propagation
description:
File: cli/internal/add/add_test.go

3 tests:
- TestHashMatchTaint_MatchesPrivateItem: create library item with matching hash and private taint -> returns private/acme-internal
- TestHashMatchTaint_NoMatch: nonexistent hash -> returns empty
- TestHashMatchTaint_PublicItemIgnored: library item with matching hash but public taint -> returns empty (only private items matched)

Uses catalog.GlobalContentDirOverride for test isolation.

title: Task 1.4: Add review step with security callouts
description:
# Review Step Before Creation

## Why
Users need to see exactly what they're about to create before committing. This is especially important for security — hooks and MCP configs define executable commands that will run on the user's system.

## Description
After destination selection and before committing, show a full summary grouped by content type. The user can confirm to create or go back to edit.

### Security Callout
If the loadout includes hooks or MCP configs (especially from registries), the review step explicitly displays commands that will be executed:

```
!! Security Notice !!
This loadout includes executable content:
  Hook: PostToolUse -> "./scripts/lint.sh"
  MCP:  my-server   -> "node server.js --port 3000"
Review these commands before installing.
Trust this code and verify it before putting it in your environment.
```

### Item Display
- Names include source in parentheses when ambiguous: concise-output (acme-registry)
- Types with >3 names that overflow modal width truncate with "+ N more"
- Empty types omitted from summary

### Completion Signal
Add confirmed bool field to modal. Create sets confirmed=true + active=false. Esc sets only active=false. App checks confirmed to distinguish completion from cancellation.

## Success Criteria
- All metadata and selections displayed in readable grouped format
- Hook/MCP commands displayed with security warning when present
- Item names show source qualifier when ambiguous across registries
- Long item lists truncated with "+ N more"
- Back returns to destination step, Create triggers doCreateLoadout()
- confirmed field correctly distinguishes completion from cancellation
- Review step scrolls if content exceeds modal height, buttons pinned to bottom
- Mouse: clickable buttons

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: review renders correct summary for mixed selection
2. Unit test: review with hooks shows security warning with command strings
3. Unit test: review with MCP shows server commands
4. Unit test: review with 0 items shows warning
5. Unit test: Back returns to dest step with state preserved
6. Unit test: Esc sets confirmed=false, Create sets confirmed=true
7. Unit test: long item list truncates with "+ N more"
8. Unit test: ambiguous names show source qualifier
9. Golden file: review step with mixed content types (80x30)
10. Golden file: review step with hooks/MCP security warning

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go (new review step)
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (check confirmed field)
close_reason: Review step enhanced: actual hook/MCP commands in security callout, ambiguous name disambiguation, +N more truncation for long type lists, scroll support with Up/Down keys. 9 new tests covering all requirements.

title: Extract rebuildRegistryState helper
description:
## Phase 1d: Extract rebuildRegistryState helper

### Problem
Three done-message handlers (registryAddDoneMsg ~line 601, registryRemoveDoneMsg ~line 623, registrySyncDoneMsg ~line 648) duplicate ~10 lines of config reload + model rebuild + catalog rescan.

### Implementation
Extract a shared helper method:

```go
func (a *App) rebuildRegistryState() {
    cfg, err := config.Load(a.catalog.RepoRoot)
    if err == nil {
        a.registryCfg = cfg
    }
    a.registries = newRegistriesModel(a.catalog.RepoRoot, a.registryCfg, a.catalog)
    a.registries.width = a.width - sidebarWidth - 1
    a.registries.height = a.panelHeight()
    a.sidebar.registryCount = len(a.registryCfg.Registries)
    cat, scanErr := catalog.ScanWithGlobalAndRegistries(a.catalog.RepoRoot, a.projectRoot, a.registrySources)
    if scanErr == nil {
        a.catalog = cat
        a.refreshSidebarCounts()
    }
}
```

Replace the duplicated blocks in all three handlers with `a.rebuildRegistryState()`.

**Cursor adjustment:** The registryRemoveDoneMsg handler also clamps `a.cardCursor` after rebuild (app.go:637-639). This stays OUTSIDE the helper — the remove handler calls `rebuildRegistryState()` then does the clamp. Add and sync handlers just call the helper directly.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 601-663 (three done-message handlers)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] New `rebuildRegistryState()` method exists on *App
- [ ] All three done-message handlers (add, remove, sync) call rebuildRegistryState()
- [ ] Remove handler still clamps a.cardCursor AFTER calling the helper
- [ ] No duplicated config reload / model rebuild / catalog rescan code remains in the handlers
- [ ] Each handler still sets its own statusMessage and registryOpInProgress before calling the helper
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not add statusIsErr logic yet (that's Phase 1a)
- Do not change what the handlers do with statusMessage — just extract the shared rebuild

title: P2-A3: Refactor TUI app.go doCreateLoadoutFromScreen to use shared functions
Provider discovery: D/select-all/add-selected buttons broken
description:
## Bug
On the "Add provider" discovery screen (stepDiscoverySelect), multiple controls are broken when the item list is large (e.g., Claude provider with many items):
- D key only toggles the current cursor line instead of deselecting all
- "Select all" only toggles the current line
- "Add selected" only toggles the current line
- All three buttons appear to do the same thing (toggle current item)
- No scrolling when the list exceeds terminal height
- Click targets are misaligned

**Note:** With smaller lists (e.g., Gemini with few items), the buttons work correctly. This suggests the bug is triggered by list overflow — likely the click zones / button zones are calculated wrong when content scrolls or overflows.

## Additional UX Issues (same flow)
- No confirmation modal before adding items ("Are you sure you want to add N items?")
- This is both a best practice and accessibility requirement

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/import.go` — stepDiscoverySelect handling, updateDiscoverySelect(), View() for stepDiscoverySelect

## Impact
Users cannot import multiple discovered items at once when the list is large. The entire discovery multi-select flow is non-functional for providers with many items.

title: Task 1.0: Add shared isValidName() for all user-entered names
description:
# Add Shared Name Validation

## Why
User-entered names become directory names via filepath.Join(destDir, name) with no character validation. Names like ../../.ssh/foo create directories outside the intended tree. This is a path traversal vulnerability that must be fixed before building the new wizard on top.

## Description
Add a shared isValidName() function used everywhere: loadout names, import names, content names. One rule, one validator, no gaps.

Restriction: [a-zA-Z0-9_-], no dots, no path separators, no leading dash, max 100 chars.

Validate in both:
- Wizard UI (immediate feedback at the name input step)
- doCreateLoadout() and manifest Parse() (defense-in-depth)

Also fix: home, _ := os.UserHomeDir() in doCreateLoadout() — check the error.

## Success Criteria
- isValidName() function exists and is used for all user-entered names
- Names with path separators, dots, or special chars are rejected with clear error message
- Existing code in doCreateLoadout(), import, and manifest.Parse() calls isValidName()
- os.UserHomeDir() error is checked in doCreateLoadout()

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: isValidName("my-loadout") returns true
2. Unit test: isValidName("../../.ssh/foo") returns false
3. Unit test: isValidName("../evil") returns false
4. Unit test: isValidName(".hidden") returns false
5. Unit test: isValidName("") returns false
6. Unit test: isValidName with 101 chars returns false
7. Unit test: wizard name step shows error for invalid names

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (doCreateLoadout)
- cli/internal/loadout/manifest.go (Parse)
- New: shared validation function (location TBD — config or model package)
close_reason: Implemented shared name validation with IsValidItemName (enhanced with leading-dash reject + 100-char max) and ValidateUserName (human-readable errors). Applied at UI layer (import wizard, create loadout wizard) and defense-in-depth (doCreateLoadoutFromScreen, manifest.Parse). Full TDD with unit and integration tests.

title: Add registryAddNonSyllagoMsg type
description:
## Phase 2a: Add registryAddNonSyllagoMsg type

### Problem
When doRegistryAdd detects a non-syllago repo with provider content (app.go:237-246), it deletes the clone and returns an error. Should instead preserve the clone and send a new message type.

### Implementation
1. Add new message type to app.go:
```go
type registryAddNonSyllagoMsg struct {
    name      string
    clonePath string
    scan      catalog.NativeScanResult
}
```

2. In doRegistryAdd (~line 237-246), where it currently detects non-syllago content and returns an error:
   - Do NOT call os.RemoveAll on the clone
   - Return a tea.Cmd that sends registryAddNonSyllagoMsg with the name, clone path, and scan result
   - Remove the error message about "use syllago add <path> instead"

3. The registryAddNonSyllagoMsg should also set registryOpInProgress = false (like other done messages do)

### Files

title: Fail-fast name validation before clone
description:
## Phase 0b: Fail-fast name validation before clone

### Problem
Name derivation (registry.NameFromURL) and duplicate/invalid checks happen inside the async command (app.go:218-228), after the modal closes and clone begins. These can be checked synchronously before the expensive network operation.

### Implementation
At app.go:1160-1171, in the handler that detects `!a.registryAddModal.active`:

1. After modal confirms (`a.registryAddModal.confirmed`), derive name from URL (or use override)
2. Check `catalog.IsValidRegistryName(name)` — if invalid, set modal error, re-open modal
3. Check for duplicates in `a.registryCfg.Registries` — if duplicate, set modal error, re-open modal
4. Only call `doRegistryAdd` if all validation passes

**Re-opening the modal on failure:**
```go
a.registryAddModal.message = fmt.Sprintf("Invalid registry name: %s", name)
a.registryAddModal.messageIsErr = true
a.registryAddModal.active = true
a.registryAddModal.confirmed = false
a.focus = focusModal  // keep input routing consistent
```
This works because modal's View() checks `if !m.active { return "" }`.

**Remove the duplicate checks from inside doRegistryAdd** (app.go:218-228) since they now happen synchronously. The async command can assume the name is valid and unique.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 1160-1171 (modal confirm handler) and 218-228 (remove redundant checks)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Invalid name shows error in modal and keeps it open (user can edit and retry)
- [ ] Duplicate name shows error in modal and keeps it open
- [ ] a.focus is set to focusModal when re-activating the modal
- [ ] doRegistryAdd no longer does name validation (moved to synchronous path)
- [ ] Empty URL validation still works (already handled by modal.go)
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not change the modal.go Update method (it already sets confirmed=true, active=false on Enter)
- Do not touch doRegistryAdd's clone/scan logic — only remove the name validation from it

Bridge registry redirect to import flow
description:
## Phase 2c: Bridge registry redirect to import flow

### Problem
When user confirms "Browse and import?" modal, need to hand off the preserved clone to the import model and switch to the import screen.

### Implementation

**1. New method on importModel (import.go):**
```go
func (m *importModel) initFromExternalClone(path string) {
    m.clonedPath = path  // takes ownership for cleanup
    m.fromRegistryRedirect = true
    m.message = ""
    m.messageIsErr = false
    m.pickCursor = 0

    cat, err := catalog.Scan(path, path)
    if err != nil || len(cat.Items) == 0 {
        // Content detected by native scan but not importable via catalog.Scan
        m.step = stepGitURL
        m.message = "Detected provider content but no importable items. Use CLI: syllago add <path>"
        m.messageIsErr = true
        m.cleanup()
        return
    }
    m.clonedItems = cat.Items
    m.step = stepGitPick
}
```

**2. Add fromRegistryRedirect field to importModel (import.go):**
```go
fromRegistryRedirect bool
```

**3. Modify Esc handler in updateGitPick (import.go ~line 709-713):**
When m.fromRegistryRedirect is true, instead of going to stepGitURL:
- Call m.cleanup()
- Clear m.fromRegistryRedirect
- Return a message (e.g., importBackToRegistriesMsg{}) that tells App.Update to switch to screenRegistries

**4. Handle confirm in App.Update (app.go):**
When the "Browse" confirmation is accepted:
```go
a.importer.cleanup()  // clean up any previous clone
a.importer.initFromExternalClone(a.pendingNonSyllagoClone)
a.pendingNonSyllagoClone = ""  // import model owns it now
a.pendingNonSyllagoScan = catalog.NativeScanResult{}
a.screen = screenImport
```

**5. Handle importBackToRegistriesMsg in App.Update:**
```go
a.screen = screenRegistries
```

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/import.go` — initFromExternalClone method, fromRegistryRedirect field, updateGitPick Esc handler
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — confirm handler, importBackToRegistriesMsg handler

### Success Criteria
- [ ] initFromExternalClone method exists on importModel
- [ ] fromRegistryRedirect bool field exists on importModel
- [ ] Confirming "Browse" switches to screenImport with items populated
- [ ] Esc from stepGitPick when fromRegistryRedirect returns to screenRegistries (not stepGitURL)
- [ ] Clone cleanup happens via import model's cleanup() on Esc
- [ ] Scan strategy mismatch handled: if catalog.Scan finds nothing, shows explanatory message and cleans up
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify native_scan.go
- Do not add new import wizard steps — reuse existing stepGitPick

title: Handle non-syllago message with confirmation modal
description:
## Phase 2b: Handle non-syllago message with confirmation modal

### Problem
registryAddNonSyllagoMsg needs a handler that shows a confirmation dialog offering to browse and import.

### Implementation
1. Add new App fields:
```go
pendingNonSyllagoClone string                  // temp clone path
pendingNonSyllagoScan  catalog.NativeScanResult // what was detected
```

2. Add handler in App.Update() for registryAddNonSyllagoMsg:
   - Set registryOpInProgress = false
   - Store msg.clonePath -> a.pendingNonSyllagoClone
   - Store msg.scan -> a.pendingNonSyllagoScan
   - Build a description of detected providers from scan results (e.g., "Found Claude Code, Cursor content")
   - Show a confirmation modal:
     - Title: "Not a Syllago Registry"
     - Body: "This repository contains [provider list] content but isn't a syllago registry.\n\nBrowse and import individual items?"
     - Two buttons: "Browse" (confirm) and "Cancel"

3. The confirmation modal can use the existing modal patterns in the codebase (check how other confirmation modals work in app.go/modal.go)

### Files

title: Hook security warnings during import
description:
WHY: The hooks deep-dive (docs/providers/audit/hooks.md lines 126-131) highlights that project-level hooks are an attack surface. A malicious .claude/settings.json in a cloned repo could execute arbitrary commands. syllago should warn when importing hooks that contain potentially dangerous commands.

RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/audit/hooks.md (Security section, lines 126-131)
RESEARCH REF: Community projects: lasso-security/claude-hooks (prompt injection defense), affaan-m/everything-claude-code (config protection hooks)

WHAT TO BUILD:
- During syllago add of hooks content, scan hook commands for:
  - Shell commands that access network (curl, wget, nc)
  - Commands that modify system state (rm, chmod, chown)
  - Commands that read secrets (cat ~/.ssh/*, env | grep)
  - Overly broad matchers (match everything)
- Produce warnings (not blocks) with explanations
- Optional --security-scan flag for deeper analysis

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Importing hooks with 'curl' commands produces a security warning
- Importing hooks with narrow matchers and safe commands produces no warning
- Warnings are informational, not blocking

TESTING: Unit tests with various hook commands
close_reason: ScanHookSecurity scanner: HIGH (network/destructive), MEDIUM (broad matchers/permissions), LOW (system paths). 20 test cases. All tests pass.

title: Fix status rendering on welcome screen
description:
## Phase 1c: Fix status rendering on welcome screen

### Problem
app.go:2103-2108 always renders statusMessage with successMsgStyle and "Done:" prefix — even for errors. In-progress messages also get the "Done:" prefix.

### Implementation
Replace the existing status rendering block (~lines 2103-2108) with:
```go
if a.statusMessage != "" {
    if a.statusIsErr {
        s += errorMsgStyle.Render(a.statusMessage) + "\n"
    } else {
        s += successMsgStyle.Render(a.statusMessage) + "\n"
    }
    for _, w := range a.statusWarnings {
        s += warningStyle.Render("Warning: " + w) + "\n"
    }
    s += "\n"
}
```

Key changes:
- Use statusIsErr to select error vs success style
- Drop the "Done:" prefix entirely (past tense messages like "Added registry: foo" already communicate completion)
- Keep warning rendering for statusWarnings

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` around lines 2103-2108 (renderContentWelcome)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Error messages render with errorMsgStyle (not green successMsgStyle)
- [ ] Success messages render with successMsgStyle
- [ ] No "Done:" prefix on any status message
- [ ] statusWarnings still render with warningStyle
- [ ] In-progress messages don't get "Done:" prefix
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes (golden files may need updating)

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not change how statusMessage is set — only how it's rendered

title: TUI: git fetch on Init() blocks terminal input when credentials unavailable
description:
## Problem

The TUI's `Init()` method (app.go:178) calls `checkForUpdate()` which runs `git fetch origin main --quiet --tags` as an async Bubble Tea Cmd. When git credentials are not available (private repo, expired token, VHS recording, CI), git prompts for `Username for 'https://github.com':` on the terminal.

This prompt **steals stdin from the TUI**, making all keyboard navigation impossible. The TUI renders but is completely frozen — no keystrokes reach Bubble Tea because the git subprocess owns the pty input.

## Root Cause

`update.go:609` — the `exec.Command("git", ...)` inherits the process's stdin/stdout/stderr. When git needs credentials, it prompts on the same terminal the TUI is using.

```go
// update.go:606-610
func checkForUpdate(repoRoot, localVersion string) tea.Cmd {
    return func() tea.Msg {
        fetchCmd := exec.Command("git", "-C", repoRoot, "fetch", "origin", "main", "--quiet", "--tags")
        fetchCmd.Run() // ← inherits stdin, blocks on credential prompt
```

## Impact

- TUI is completely unusable when credentials aren't cached
- Affects VHS/demo recordings (no credential helper available)
- Affects CI/CD environments
- Affects users who cloned via HTTPS without a credential helper
- No timeout — hangs indefinitely

## Recommended Fix

Three changes in `update.go`, `checkForUpdate()`:

### 1. Suppress credential prompts (one-line fix)
```go
fetchCmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0")
```
This makes git fail silently instead of prompting. The existing `fetchCmd.Run()` already ignores errors, so this is safe.

### 2. Detach from terminal
```go
fetchCmd.Stdin = nil
fetchCmd.Stdout = nil  
fetchCmd.Stderr = nil
```
Prevents git from ever touching the TUI's terminal.

### 3. Add a timeout (defense in depth)
```go
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
defer cancel()
fetchCmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "git", "-C", repoRoot, "fetch", "origin", "main", "--quiet", "--tags")
```

## Additional Consideration

The `registryAutoSync` setting exists in settings.go but registry sync is user-triggered (press `s`). The `Init()` update check is NOT registry sync — it's a version check against the syllago repo itself. Consider:
- Throttling to once per day (cache last check timestamp)
- Making it a setting (like `registryAutoSync`) that users can disable
- Showing a non-blocking notification instead of blocking launch

## Files

- `cli/internal/tui/update.go` — lines 606-610 (`checkForUpdate`)
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — line 178 (`Init()`)

## How to Reproduce

```bash
# Record with VHS (no credential helper in pty)
HOME=/tmp/clean-home vhs demo.tape

# Or run with expired/missing credentials
GIT_ASKPASS=false syl
# TUI renders but keyboard input is frozen
```
close_reason: Fixed git fetch stdin steal + added Update check setting

title: Add 'syllago add --from shared' for bundled content
description:
WHY: Syllago bundles skills in content/skills/ (syllago-guide, syllago-import, syllago-provider-audit, etc.) but there's no workflow to get them into the user's library. syllago add requires --from <provider>, and shared content isn't a provider. Users who clone a repo with bundled content can see it in syllago list ([shared] tag) but can't install or convert it.

USE CASE: User clones syllago, runs make build, wants to use the built-in syllago-provider-audit skill. Today they're stuck — no command path from shared content to library to installed.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- syllago add --from shared: discovers content in the repo's content/ directory and adds to library
- syllago add <name> --from shared: adds a specific item
- syllago add --from shared --all: adds all bundled content
- The shared content dir is already scanned by catalog (shows as [shared] in list) — the gap is in the add command's provider requirement

AFFECTS: Any repo that bundles syllago content, not just the syllago repo itself. Teams could put skills in their project's content/ dir.

IMPLEMENTATION HINTS:
- cli/cmd/syllago/add_cmd.go: the --from flag validates against provider.AllProviders — need to also accept "shared"
- When --from shared, scan the project's content/ dir instead of a provider's config dir
- The rest of the add flow (copy to library, metadata creation) should work as-is

title: Task 1.2: Add content type selection step to wizard
description:
# Add Content Type Selection Step

## Why
Before walking through individual content types, users should select which types they want in their loadout. This reduces friction — most users want all types (default checked), power users can uncheck types they don't need.

## Description
New step inserted between provider selection and per-type item selection. Shows checkboxes for each content type supported by the selected provider, defaulting all to checked. Only types with available items in the catalog are shown.

Microcopy: "Uncheck any content types you want to skip."

Keys:
- Space: toggle individual checkbox
- a: Select All / Deselect All toggle
- Enter: proceed to first per-type item step
- Esc: go back to provider step

Mouse: clicking checkbox or type label toggles it.

## Success Criteria
- After selecting a provider, checkbox screen shows all supported content types with available items, all checked by default
- Space toggles individual, a toggles all
- Enter proceeds, Esc goes back
- Selecting 0 types + Enter shows "Select at least one content type"
- Mouse clicks toggle checkboxes
- Modal fits at 60x20 terminal (height capped to min(24, termHeight-2))

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: given provider supporting Rules/Skills/Agents but catalog has only Rules and Skills, verify only Rules and Skills shown, both checked
2. Unit test: a key toggles all on/off
3. Unit test: 0 types + Enter shows validation error
4. Unit test: Space toggles individual type
5. Unit test: Esc goes back to provider step
6. Unit test: mouse click on checkbox toggles it
7. Golden file: wizard at content type selection step (80x30)
8. Golden file: wizard at content type selection step (60x20) — verify modal height cap

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go (new step in wizard)
- cli/internal/tui/keys.go (if new bindings needed)

title: Private repo content locking
description: Content from private repos should be locked to their origin registry — cannot be added to a different registry, can only be updated within its current registry. Prevents unauthorized redistribution of private content.
close_reason: Implemented as part of the registry privacy gate system. Content from private registries is tainted at import time and blocked from being published/shared to public registries (Gates G1-G4). See commits 54b3842..291f18b.

title: Registry card: clicking truncated URL navigates to truncated URL

## Bug
When a registry URL is truncated with "..." in the card display, clicking it opens the truncated URL (with the "...") instead of the actual full URL. The truncation is visual-only but the click handler uses the displayed text.

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/registries.go` — renderRegistryCard() URL truncation and zone handling

## Observed
Click on truncated URL like "https://github.com/owner/re..." opens that literal string.

## Expected
Click should use the full URL from the entry data, not the truncated display string.

title: Task 3: Create session-handoff-read.ts hook
description:
Create the SessionStart hook that reads .handoff.json from the project root and injects it as a context block into Claude's session.

Files to create: ~/.config/pai/hooks/session-handoff-read.ts

Key design decisions:
- Resolves project root by walking up from cwd looking for .git/ or .handoff.json
- Outputs via console.log (stdout) — same pattern as load-core-context.ts
- Skips if file doesn't exist (first session), is malformed, or project root can't be determined
- Skips for subagent sessions (same guard as load-core-context.ts)
- Reads SessionStart payload from stdin (JSON with session_id, cwd fields)

Output format: <system-reminder> block with formatted handoff sections (ACTIVE WORK, BEADS, GIT STATE, RECENT DECISIONS, NEXT STEPS, NOTES, PREVIOUS SESSIONS)

Success criteria: echo '{"session_id":"test","cwd":"/home/hhewett/.local/src/syllago"}' | bun run ~/.config/pai/hooks/session-handoff-read.ts — exits cleanly with no output when no .handoff.json exists; outputs formatted context block when it does exist.

Plan ref: docs/plans/2026-03-17-session-handoff-implementation.md, Task 3

title: Task 1.1: Diagnose and fix loadout creation bug
description:
# Diagnose and Fix Loadout Creation Bug

## Why
Loadouts created via the wizard don't appear in the TUI after creation. This is the most basic prerequisite — the feature is broken.

## Description
The doCreateLoadoutMsg handler (app.go:835) rescans the catalog and refreshes sidebar counts, but the loadout still doesn't show. Possible causes:
- Catalog scanner not finding the new loadout directory
- Path mismatch (writing to a location the scanner doesn't search)
- Sidebar/card refresh issue after catalog rescan

## Success Criteria
- Create a loadout via the wizard -> it appears immediately in the Loadouts card view
- Sidebar count updates to reflect the new loadout
- Works for both Project and Library destinations
- Toast shows success message with loadout name

## Test-Driven Development
Write tests FIRST, then fix:
1. Unit test: create loadout via doCreateLoadout() with a temp catalog, verify doCreateLoadoutMsg triggers catalog rescan that includes the new loadout
2. Integration test: full wizard flow (provider -> items -> name -> dest -> confirm) -> verify loadout appears in rendered output
3. Verify golden files for loadout cards view still pass

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/app.go (lines 345-410, 835-846)
- cli/internal/tui/loadout_create.go
- cli/internal/catalog/scanner.go
close_reason: Bug diagnosed and fixed. The handler code at app.go:932 was already correct (rescan + navigate to detail). Fixed Library destination path to use catalog.GlobalContentDir() instead of hardcoded os.UserHomeDir() path (single source of truth). Added 2 integration tests: TestCreateLoadoutHandlerNavigatesToDetail (full handler flow verifying detail nav, toast, sidebar count) and TestCreateLoadoutLibraryDestination (Library dest via GlobalContentDirOverride). All tests pass.

title: Preserve updatedInput and structured output fields in hook conversion
description:
WHY: Claude Code hooks can return structured JSON output including updatedInput (transparent arg rewriting), suppressOutput, systemMessage, additionalContext, continue, and permissionDecision. These fields are critical for security hooks. Our converter preserves the hook definition but doesn't model or warn about these runtime capabilities.

RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/audit/hooks.md (lines 30-31 — structured output fields)
RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/claude-code/hooks.md (exit codes and structured output)
RELATED: syllago-qumq (hook matcher regex handling) — could be combined

WHAT TO DO:
- Document which structured output fields each provider supports
- During hook conversion, if a hook's purpose implies arg rewriting (e.g., command rewrites tool input), warn when exporting to providers that don't support updatedInput
- Consider adding a 'capabilities' annotation to canonical hook entries

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Converter warns about capability mismatches for structured output
- Documentation of per-provider structured output support

TESTING: Unit tests for warning conditions
close_reason: HookOutputCapabilities map for 6 providers. SourceProvider propagation in canonical. OutputFieldsLostWarnings generates mismatch warnings. 8 new tests. All tests pass.

title: Task 2.5: Add pane navigation for split view
description:
# Pane Navigation for Split View

## Why
Users need to navigate between file tree and preview panes. Tab behavior must stay consistent with the rest of the app (sidebar/content toggle).

## Description
Navigation model:
- Tab / Shift+Tab: toggles sidebar and content area (consistent globally)
- l or Right: from file tree, move focus to preview pane
- h or Left: from preview pane, move focus back to tree
- 1 / 2: switch tabs (Files | Install) from anywhere in content
- Arrow keys: navigate within focused pane (Up/Down for tree cursor or preview scroll)

### Focus State
Zone focus lives on detailModel (not App):
```go
type detailZone int
const (
    zoneFileTree detailZone = iota
    zonePreview
)
```
App decides sidebar vs content vs modal. detailModel decides which pane.

### Focus Indicator
Pane title in accent color when focused, muted when unfocused. No full border (saves width).

### Behavior
- Default focus on file tree when entering detail view
- Switching tabs resets focus to file tree
- Tab to sidebar, Tab back lands on last-focused pane
- Help bar: "l/right preview . h/left tree . 1/2 tabs . tab sidebar"

## Success Criteria
- Tab toggles sidebar/content (same as all other pages)
- l/Right moves tree -> preview, h/Left moves preview -> tree
- 1/2 switch tabs from anywhere
- Focus indicator visible (accent vs muted pane title)
- Help bar shows pane navigation keys
- Default focus on file tree on enter
- Focus resets on tab switch

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: Tab toggles sidebar/content (NOT pane cycling)
2. Unit test: l from tree focuses preview, h from preview focuses tree
3. Unit test: 1/2 switch tabs
4. Unit test: arrows work within focused pane only
5. Unit test: entering detail focuses file tree
6. Unit test: switching tabs resets focus to tree
7. Golden file: tree focused vs preview focused

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/detail.go (detailZone, focus routing)
- cli/internal/tui/keys.go (new bindings if needed)
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go (focus indicator rendering)
close_reason: Already completed in earlier commits (86cb395, f6b7935)

title: Task 3.1: Split view for loadout Contents tab
description:
# Loadout Contents Split View

## Why
The Contents tab currently shows a static list of items. Users can't preview what's in a loadout without navigating away. A split view lets users evaluate contents in-place.

## Description
Reuses splitViewModel from Phase 2 (Task 2.4). Left pane shows grouped items, right pane shows preview of selected item's primary file.

### Left Pane
- Items grouped by type with headers: "Rules (2)", "Skills (3)", etc.
- Item names show source: concise-output (acme-registry) or (library)
- Type headers are non-selectable — splitViewModel skips disabled items on cursor movement
- First item selected on entry

### Right Pane
- Preview of highlighted item's primary file (same logic as Phase 2)
- Glamour for markdown, plain for others, cached

### Item Resolution
- Resolves manifest references against catalog to find ContentItem objects
- Unresolved items shown with warning indicator and "(not found)" in preview

### Single-Pane Fallback
Same as Phase 2: content width < 70 collapses to single pane with Enter/Esc replace-in-place.

## Success Criteria
- Contents tab shows split view with grouped items and preview
- Cursor skips type headers
- Preview updates on cursor movement
- Item sources shown (registry name or "library")
- Unresolved items shown with warning
- Single-pane collapse at narrow widths
- Read-only — no actions from preview (drill-in is roadmap)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: items grouped by type with counts
2. Unit test: cursor skips type headers
3. Unit test: preview shows primary file of selected item
4. Unit test: unresolved item shows "(not found)"
5. Unit test: item source labels (registry name, "library")
6. Unit test: single-pane collapse at 60x20
7. Golden files at all size variants
8. Integration: navigate to kitchen-sink loadout, verify contents render with previews

## Key Files
- cli/internal/tui/detail_render.go (loadout Contents tab)
- cli/internal/tui/splitview.go (reused from Phase 2)
- cli/internal/loadout/resolve.go (reference resolution)

title: 2.1: Create visibility.go with platform detection logic
description:
File: cli/internal/registry/visibility.go (new)

Implement ProbeVisibility(rawURL string) string — platform-aware detector returning public/private/unknown. Priority: API probe > registry.yaml > default unknown.

Functions to implement:
- ProbeVisibility() — dispatches to platform-specific probes
- ResolveVisibility(probed, declared) — stricter value wins (private > unknown > public)
- stricterOf(a, b) — ordering comparison
- IsPrivate(visibility) — true for private, unknown, empty
- NeedsReprobe(checkedAt *time.Time) — TTL check (1 hour)
- parseGitURL(rawURL) — extract platform/owner/repo from HTTPS and SSH URLs
- platformFromHost(host) — map host to github/gitlab/bitbucket/unknown
- probeGitHub/probeGitLab/probeBitbucket — HTTP API probes
- parseGitHubResponse/parseGitLabResponse/parseBitbucketResponse — extracted JSON parsers
- OverrideProbeForTest var for test injection
- visibilityHTTPClient var with 5s timeout

Add VisibilityCacheTTL = time.Hour constant.

Commit: feat(registry): add visibility.go — platform-aware GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket probe

title: 2.2: Write tests for visibility.go
description:
File: cli/internal/registry/visibility_test.go (new)

Unit tests using httptest.Server mocks — no real network calls. Tests:
- TestParseGitURL — table-driven for HTTPS, SSH, .git suffix, all platforms, unknown host, invalid URL
- TestStricterOf — all combinations of private/unknown/public
- TestIsPrivate — private, unknown, empty -> true; public -> false
- TestNeedsReprobe — nil (true), fresh (false), stale 2h ago (true)
- TestProbeGitHub_Public/Private — via parseGitHubResponse helper
- TestProbeGitHub_404/429 — via rewriteTransport redirecting to httptest.Server
- TestProbeGitLab_Response — public, private, internal, other
- TestProbeBitbucket_Response — is_private true/false
- TestProbeVisibility_UnknownHost — returns unknown
- TestResolveVisibility_StricterWins — 4 scenarios
- rewriteTransport helper type for redirecting HTTP to test server

Commit: test(registry): visibility detection unit tests with httptest mocks

title: 2.3: Wire visibility probe into registry add/sync commands
description:
File: cli/cmd/syllago/registry_cmd.go

After registry.Clone() succeeds in the add handler: probe visibility via registry.ProbeVisibility(url), load manifest for declared visibility, merge with ResolveVisibility(), save Visibility and VisibilityCheckedAt to config.

In the sync handler: after successful pull, re-probe if NeedsReprobe(r.VisibilityCheckedAt) returns true. Update config with fresh visibility and timestamp.

Read registry_cmd.go fully before editing to understand exact function names and config loading patterns.

title: Post-add success message: poor placement and warning overflow
description:
## Bug
After adding items via provider discovery (e.g., "Add provider → Gemini → add selected"):

1. **Success message placement**: The "Added [item]" message with the full list of added items renders at the very top of the welcome/landing page. When many items are added, this creates a long block of text that pushes the actual content down. It's not obvious and looks bad.

2. **Warning overflow**: After the success message, a large number of warnings scatter across the screen. The warnings are not contained or organized — they just dump all over the visible area.

## Expected
- Success message should be in a scrollable modal or summary panel, not inline at the top of the landing page
- Warnings should be grouped and contained (e.g., collapsible section, or "N warnings" with expand option)
- For bulk adds, a summary ("Added 12 items to library") with optional detail expansion

## Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — statusMessage / statusWarnings rendering in renderContentWelcome()
- The status message system renders a flat string + flat warning list — doesn't scale to bulk operations

## Context
This is the same statusMessage system we just improved (statusIsErr, keypress clearing). The core rendering works for single-item operations but breaks down for bulk adds with many warnings.

title: [Epic] Tool Coverage: Zed Provider
Render status messages on registries screen
description:
## Phase 1b: Render status messages on registries screen

### Problem
statusMessage only renders in renderContentWelcome() (the category landing page). Registry add/remove/sync operations happen on screenRegistries, where the user sees zero feedback.

### Implementation
1. Change registries.go View() signature from `View(cursor int)` to `View(cursor int, statusMsg string, statusIsErr bool)`
2. Render status between breadcrumb and card grid:
```go
if statusMsg != "" {
    if statusIsErr {
        s += errorMsgStyle.Render(statusMsg) + "\n\n"
    } else {
        s += successMsgStyle.Render(statusMsg) + "\n\n"
    }
}
```
3. Update call site at app.go (~line 1906) to pass `a.statusMessage, a.statusIsErr`

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/registries.go` — View() method
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — call site (~line 1906)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] View() accepts statusMsg and statusIsErr parameters
- [ ] Error messages render with errorMsgStyle on registries screen
- [ ] Success messages render with successMsgStyle on registries screen
- [ ] Empty statusMsg renders nothing (no blank lines)
- [ ] Call site passes a.statusMessage and a.statusIsErr
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes (golden files may need updating)

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not use strings.Builder optimization (deferred to accessibility pass)
- Do not add text-based selection indicators to cards (deferred)

title: Synthetic review findings
[Epic] Tool Coverage: Roo Code Provider
KS P2: Claude Code fixtures
Bug: syllago add only copies primary content file, drops supporting files
description:
When adding a skill (or other content type) that has supporting files in subdirectories, syllago add only copies the primary file (e.g., SKILL.md) and ignores everything else (workflows/, scripts/, references/, etc.).

This means:
1. Library copy is incomplete — skill won't work correctly without its reference files
2. syllago publish also only publishes the single file (since library is the source)
3. Users silently lose content with no warning

Reproduction:
- Have a skill with subdirectory files: skills/atlassian/SKILL.md + skills/atlassian/workflows/**
- Run: syllago add skills/atlassian --from claude-code
- Check library: only SKILL.md is present, workflows/ directory is missing

Expected: All files in the skill directory tree should be copied to the library.

Root cause: The add workflow (internal/add package) likely uses single-file copy logic rather than directory-recursive copy. Need to trace AddItems() and DiscoverFromProvider() to find where files are enumerated.

Impact: Any multi-file skill or content item is silently broken after add. This affects the entire add → library → publish pipeline.
close_reason: Fixed: added SourceDir field to DiscoveryItem, copySupportingFiles() in writeItem(), 4 new tests

GitHub Copilot CLI's programmatic mode needs research before we can write smoke tests. Deferred from the initial E2E test suite.

## When Ready
Follow the same pattern as Claude Code and Gemini CLI smoke tests.

title: 3.3: Refactor App.Update() for Focus-Based Input Routing
description:
Refactors App.Update() so keyboard input is routed based on the focus field rather than screen state: sidebar keys go to sidebarModel when focusSidebar, Enter/Right loads items into the content panel, and Esc from items shifts focus back to sidebar without changing screen.

Files:
- Modify: cli/internal/tui/app.go (lines 77-509 Update method)
- Modify: cli/internal/tui/keys.go (add Right key.Binding)

Steps:
1. Add 'Right key.Binding' to keyMap struct and initialize with keys 'right', 'l' in keys.go
2. Update WindowSizeMsg handler to compute contentW = msg.Width - sidebarWidth - 1 and propagate to all sub-models
3. Add Tab/ShiftTab panel-focus toggle before screen-specific handling (guard: not on screenDetail, not in search/help overlay)
4. Replace all a.category.selectedType() references in the search-active block with a.sidebar.selectedType()
5. Replace case screenCategory: with sidebar-focus routing: Enter/Right loads items into content and sets focus = focusContent; Up/Down navigates sidebar
6. Update case screenItems: Esc to set focus = focusSidebar instead of changing screen to screenCategory
7. Add a.focus = focusSidebar to screenImport/Update/Settings Esc handlers

Plan reference: docs/plans/2026-02-17-tui-redesign-implementation.md, Task 3.3

Done when:
- Right key.Binding added to keyMap struct and initialized with keys 'right', 'l'
- Tab key toggles focus between sidebar and content (guarded for screenDetail)
- All input routes to modal first when modal active
- When focusSidebar: j/k/up/down navigate sidebar, Enter/Right loads items into content and shifts focus to content
- When focusContent: existing screen-specific key handlers fire
- Esc from items view shifts focus back to sidebar
- WindowSizeMsg propagates content width to sub-models (width - sidebarWidth)

title: Fix canonical hook timeout unit inconsistency
description:
WHY: Claude Code stores hook timeouts in seconds, Copilot canonicalizer stores milliseconds. Round-tripping a hook through syllago produces wrong timeout values depending on source provider. This is a data corruption bug.

FINDINGS:
- CC-4: hooks.go:296-297 (Claude Code seconds), hooks.go:434 (Copilot ms conversion)

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Define canonical timeout unit explicitly (recommend seconds, matching Claude Code)
- All canonicalizers normalize to the same unit
- All renderers convert from canonical to provider-specific unit
- Round-trip: Claude Code -> canonical -> Copilot preserves correct timeout
- Round-trip: Copilot -> canonical -> Claude Code preserves correct timeout

TESTING:
- Unit: Test canonicalize from each provider with known timeout values
- Unit: Test render to each provider produces correct unit
- Unit: Round-trip test across providers

DOCS (syllago-docs/): Document canonical timeout unit in format spec
close_reason: Canonical hook timeout defined as seconds, all providers aligned

[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. bd sync                 (commit beads changes)
[ ] 3.5. /handoff              (write .handoff.json for next session)

[ ] 6. git push                (push to remote)

title: Task 2.1: Remove README.md infrastructure from codebase
description:
# Remove README.md Infrastructure

## Why
README.md is an unnecessary layer of indirection. Skills have SKILL.md, agents are markdown files, hooks are configs — the content IS the documentation. Removing README simplifies authoring and reduces code complexity (parsing, rendering, warnings for missing README).

## Description
Remove all README.md handling from the codebase:

- catalog/scanner.go: Remove loadReadme(), readDescription() calls for README.md, README.md warnings, ReadmeBody field population
- catalog/types.go: Remove ReadmeBody field from ContentItem
- cli/internal/readme/: Delete entire package
- catalog/scanner.go: Update description extraction to use primary content files (SKILL.md frontmatter, first paragraph of agent .md, etc.)
- scanner.go:362: Remove README.md from isMetaFile() skip list — README.md becomes a regular file in the file tree

Note: Removing README.md from isMetaFile() means any README.md files in user-authored content directories will start appearing in the file tree. Call out in PR description.

## Success Criteria
- ReadmeBody field removed from ContentItem struct
- readme package deleted
- No code references README.md as a special file
- Content descriptions still populated from primary content files
- make test passes (after updating dependent tests)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: scanner discovers content items without README.md files
2. Unit test: description extracted from SKILL.md frontmatter description field

title: Full TUI codebase review: efficiency, accessibility, best practices
description:
## Goal
Comprehensive review of the full TUI codebase (cli/internal/tui/) by Go CLI expert agents, evaluating against current best practices for:
- Efficiency and code organization (state management, rendering, TEA architecture)
- CLI best practices (error messages, user feedback, progressive disclosure)
- CLI accessibility (NO_COLOR, screen readers, keyboard nav, color-independent meaning)
- TUI accessibility (focus management, modal patterns, status regions, form inputs)
- TUI patterns and best practices (modals, async UX, navigation, card layouts)

## Requirements
- Spin up research agents for each topic area to gather current (2024-2026) authoritative sources
- Feed research into Go expert agents that review the actual codebase files
- Produce both high-level pattern findings AND specific file:line recommendations
- Review scope: all files in cli/internal/tui/ plus supporting packages they depend on

## Success Criteria
- Each topic area has a structured findings document with citations
- Actionable recommendations are tied to specific code locations
- Findings are saved to docs/ for reference during implementation
- Critical issues (accessibility failures, UX anti-patterns) are separated from nice-to-haves
close_reason: Review complete: all research and findings docs exist in docs/reviews/ and docs/research-*. Implementation plan with 68 items across 6 phases documented. Many items already implemented in prior sessions.

title: Task E2E: Provider smoke tests (Claude Code + Gemini CLI)
description:
# Provider Smoke Tests

## Why
File-level tests verify that loadout apply puts files in the right places. But only real CLI tools can confirm the content is actually picked up. These smoke tests catch provider path changes, config parsing quirks, and permission issues.

## Description
Separate CI workflow that installs Claude Code and Gemini CLI, applies loadouts, and uses programmatic mode (-p flag) to verify content is loaded.

### CI Workflow
- Runs on release branches and manual trigger only (not every PR)
- Gates releases
- Separate jobs per provider
- Auth: SSO/service account (setup TBD)

### Claude Code Tests
- Apply kitchen-sink loadout, verify rules active via claude -p
- Verify MCP servers via claude mcp list (deterministic, preferred)
- Verify commands available
- Clean removal verification
- Edge cases: first-time setup, merge with existing config, sequential loadouts

### Gemini CLI Tests
- Apply loadout, verify rules via gemini -p
- Verify MCP config in settings.json
- Clean removal

### Security
- Don't log full CLI output (prevent secret leakage)
- Pin test content to known-good commits
- assert_contains uses grep -qF (fixed-string, no eval)
- Prefer deterministic introspection commands over LLM-based assertions

## Success Criteria
- CI workflow exists at .github/workflows/smoke-test-providers.yml
- Claude Code smoke tests pass
- Gemini CLI smoke tests pass
- Edge case tests pass (first-time, merge, sequential)
- No secrets leaked in CI logs

## Test-Driven Development
1. Write test scripts first (tests/smoke/claude-code.sh, tests/smoke/gemini-cli.sh)
2. Implement assert_contains with grep -qF
3. Test locally before wiring into CI
4. Add CI workflow

## Key Files
- tests/smoke/claude-code.sh (NEW)
- tests/smoke/gemini-cli.sh (NEW)
- .github/workflows/smoke-test-providers.yml (NEW)

## Open Questions
- CI authentication: how to persist SSO/OAuth on runners
- "Try" mode: verify SessionEnd hook injection rather than actual revert
close_reason: Created E2E provider smoke test suite: tests/smoke/helpers.sh (shared framework with assert_contains using grep -qF, temp HOME isolation, assertion failure tracking), tests/smoke/claude-code.sh (7 tests: first-time apply, clean removal, merge with existing config, sequential loadouts, double-apply prevention, MCP introspection, preview mode), tests/smoke/gemini-cli.sh (4 tests: cross-provider apply, clean removal, merge with existing, sequential loadouts + 3 auth-gated). CI workflow at .github/workflows/smoke-test-providers.yml with separate jobs per provider. Discovered MCP merge bug (syllago-0sbe).

title: Task 1.6: Add JSON validity check for settings file merges
description:
# Add JSON Validity Check for Settings File Merges

## Why
readJSONFileOrEmpty() doesn't validate that the file contains valid JSON before sjson operates on it. If settings.json is malformed (truncated, hand-edited with syntax error), sjson silently produces malformed output or returns a cryptic error. The snapshot protects against data loss but the error path is confusing.

## Description
Add json.Valid() check after reading the settings file in readJSONFileOrEmpty(). If invalid, return a clear error: "settings.json contains invalid JSON; fix or delete the file before applying a loadout."

## Success Criteria
- readJSONFileOrEmpty() checks json.Valid() before returning file contents
- Malformed JSON returns clear, actionable error message
- Valid JSON files continue to work as before
- Missing files still return "{}" (no change to that behavior)

## Test-Driven Development
1. Unit test: valid JSON file returns contents
2. Unit test: missing file returns "{}"
3. Unit test: malformed JSON returns error with descriptive message
4. Unit test: empty file returns error (not valid JSON)
5. Unit test: truncated JSON returns error

## Key Files
- cli/internal/loadout/apply.go (readJSONFileOrEmpty, ~line 463)
close_reason: Added json.Valid() check to readJSONFileOrEmpty() with clear error message. 5 unit tests covering valid, missing, malformed, empty, and truncated JSON.

title: Fix: install command ignores per-type path overrides from config
description:
## Problem

The `syllago install` command (`cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go`) constructs a PathResolver at line 90 but only uses `resolver.BaseDir()` at line 140, passing a flat `baseDir` string to `installer.Install()`. This means per-type config path overrides (e.g., `provider_paths.claude-code.paths.skills = "/custom/skills"`) have NO effect on `syllago install`.

The resolver priority chain is: per-type path > CLI --base-dir > config baseDir > default. Currently `install` only honors the bottom three tiers. The `sync-and-export` and `loadout apply` commands both use the resolver correctly.

## Root Cause

`installer.Install()` (cli/internal/installer/installer.go:163) accepts `baseDir string` — a flat directory override. It always calls `prov.InstallDir(baseDir, ct)` internally. There's no way to pass "use this exact install directory" without changing the function signature.

The resolver's per-type path returns a final install directory directly (bypassing `prov.InstallDir()` entirely), but `Install()` has no mechanism to accept that.

## Fix Approach

Add `InstallWithResolver(item, prov, repoRoot, method, resolver)` to `cli/internal/installer/installer.go` that:
1. Checks `IsJSONMerge` first (unchanged — merge types don't use resolver)
2. Uses `resolver.InstallDir(prov, item.Type, home)` to get the effective install dir
3. Computes the target path from that resolved dir (same logic as `CheckStatusWithResolver`)
4. Proceeds with symlink/copy/converter logic using the resolved path

Update `install_cmd.go` to call `InstallWithResolver` instead of `Install` with `resolver.BaseDir()`.

Keep existing `Install()` unchanged for backward compatibility (loadout apply and other callers that pass baseDir directly).

## Key Files

- `cli/cmd/syllago/install_cmd.go` — line 140 is the call site
- `cli/internal/installer/installer.go` — `Install()` at line 163, `CheckStatusWithResolver()` at line 114 (reference for resolver usage pattern)
- `cli/internal/config/resolver.go` — `InstallDir()` method

## Success Criteria

1. `syllago config paths set claude-code --type skills --path /custom/skills` followed by `syllago install my-skill --to claude-code` installs the skill to `/custom/skills/my-skill`, not `~/.claude/skills/my-skill`
2. Existing `--base-dir` flag still works
3. Config-level `base_dir` still works
4. Per-type path > CLI --base-dir > config baseDir > default priority chain holds
5. JSON merge types (hooks, MCP) are unaffected
6. Unit test for `InstallWithResolver` covering per-type path override
7. L2 command test verifying `install` command respects config per-type paths
8. All existing tests pass (no regressions)
close_reason: Added InstallWithResolver() to installer.go, updated install_cmd.go to use it. 7 new unit tests + 1 L2 integration test. All 8 success criteria validated.

title: Loadout UX Redesign: Phase 2 - Remove README + Split View
description:
# Phase 2: Remove README + Files Tab Split View

## Why
README.md is an unnecessary layer of indirection for syllago content. The content IS the documentation (SKILL.md, agent .md files, hook configs). Removing it simplifies authoring and the codebase. The Overview tab is replaced by merging into the Files tab with a split-view layout.

## Scope
- Remove all README.md infrastructure (scanner, types, readme package)
- Delete all README.md files from content/
- Remove Overview tab from detail view
- Add split-view to Files tab (reusable splitViewModel component)
- Add pane navigation (l/h for pane switching, Tab stays sidebar/content)

## Plan Reference
Full details in docs/loadout-ux-redesign.md (Phase 2, Tasks 2.1-2.5)

title: Task 1.2: Fix parseHookFile to resolve content file from directory
Fix data races in registry add and remove
description:
## Phase 0a: Fix data races in registry add and remove

### Problem
Both doRegistryAdd (app.go:216-258) and doRegistryRemove (app.go:191-205) capture `cfg := a.registryCfg` (a pointer) then mutate `cfg.Registries` in a goroutine. Since cfg points to a.registryCfg, View() can read the slice concurrently — data race.

### Implementation
**Strategy:** Inside each command closure, load a fresh config from disk, modify the fresh copy, save. Never touch a.registryCfg from the goroutine.

**Remove (app.go:191-205):**
1. Pass only the registry name (string) into the closure — not the cfg pointer
2. Inside closure: `freshCfg, err := config.Load(repoRoot)`
3. Filter out the registry name from freshCfg.Registries
4. Save freshCfg
5. Do filesystem removal (registry.Remove)
6. If config.Load fails, return registryRemoveDoneMsg with the error

**Add (app.go:216-258):**
1. After cloning and scanning, load fresh config inside the closure
2. Append the new registry entry to the fresh config's Registries slice
3. Save the fresh config
4. Return registryAddDoneMsg as before
5. If config.Load fails, return registryAddDoneMsg with the error

**Concurrency note:** registryOpInProgress prevents concurrent registry ops from UI, so no TOCTOU risk between Load and Save.

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` lines 191-205 (remove) and 216-258 (add)

### Success Criteria
- [ ] Remove closure does NOT capture or reference `cfg` or `a.registryCfg` — only passes registry name as string
- [ ] Add closure does NOT mutate `a.registryCfg.Registries` — loads fresh config inside closure
- [ ] Both closures call config.Load() inside the goroutine, modify the fresh copy, and save
- [ ] config.Load failure returns done message with error (not silently swallowed)
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes
- [ ] No `go vet` race warnings (existing tests should cover this)

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not modify done-message handlers (those are Phase 1d)
- Do not change the message types or their fields

title: Cross-provider skill hook conversion
description:
When converting Claude Code skills with hooks frontmatter to other providers, map skill-scoped hooks to the target provider's hook system where possible.

Current state: hooks and effort fields are preserved in SkillMeta (canonical format) and survive Claude-to-Claude roundtrips. For non-Claude providers, hooks are embedded as prose notes explaining they require skill-scoped hook support.

Future work:
- Map Claude skill hooks to Gemini CLI global hooks in settings.json (lose skill scoping but hooks actually execute)
- Map hook event types between providers (e.g., pre_tool_use → BeforeTool)
- Handle scoping semantics (Claude: per-skill, Gemini: global, Copilot: none)
- Decide on warning/confirmation UX when hook scoping is lost

Research notes:
- Gemini CLI has 10 hook event types, configured in settings.json, all global scope
- Copilot CLI has no hook system
- Kiro has hooks but different event model
- The core challenge is that hooks are executable behavior, not instructional text — prose embedding preserves intent but hooks won't fire
close_reason: Skill hooks surfaced as actionable warnings for providers with hook support. Providers without hooks keep prose embedding. Tests pass.

title: Phase 4: CLI Fixes
Add statusIsErr field to App struct
description:
## Phase 1a: Add statusIsErr field to App struct

### Problem
statusMessage has no error/success flag. All status messages render with successMsgStyle and "Done:" prefix — even errors like "Add failed: not a syllago registry...".

### Implementation
1. Add `statusIsErr bool` field to App struct (near statusMessage at ~line 103)
2. Set `statusIsErr = true` on all error paths in done-message handlers
3. Set `statusIsErr = false` on all success paths in done-message handlers

After Phase 1d extraction, the status-setting happens in each handler before calling rebuildRegistryState(). Find every place statusMessage is set and add the corresponding statusIsErr assignment:
- Error paths (where message contains "failed" or similar): statusIsErr = true
- Success paths: statusIsErr = false
- In-progress messages (like "Adding registry: ..."): statusIsErr = false

### Files
- `cli/internal/tui/app.go` — App struct definition and all done-message handlers

### Success Criteria
- [ ] `statusIsErr bool` field exists on App struct
- [ ] Every assignment to a.statusMessage is accompanied by an assignment to a.statusIsErr
- [ ] Error messages get statusIsErr = true
- [ ] Success messages get statusIsErr = false
- [ ] In-progress messages get statusIsErr = false
- [ ] `make build && make test` passes

### DO NOT
- Do not mark this bead as complete — a verification agent will do that
- Do not change how statusMessage is rendered yet (that's Phases 1b and 1c)
- Do not add clearing logic yet (that's Phase 3)

Remove Overview tab from all content type detail views:

- Mouse zones: Update tab-N zone IDs for new indices

- Tab switching keys: 1/2 instead of 1/2/3

title: Release workflow: tag-triggered build + sign + publish
Hook portability report: warn about capability mismatches during install/export
description:
WHY: Different providers have vastly different hook capabilities. A hook using updatedInput (arg rewriting) only works on Claude Code and Gemini CLI — Windsurf/Cursor silently ignore the rewrite. A hook using 'prompt' handler type only works on Claude Code. Users exporting hooks cross-provider get no warning about these capability losses.

RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/audit/hooks.md (capability matrix on lines 46-59)
RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/claude-code/hooks.md (4 handler types)
RESEARCH REF: docs/providers/windsurf/hooks.md (command-only handlers)

WHAT TO BUILD:
- During syllago install/export of hooks, analyze hook entries for provider-specific features
- Warn about: handler types not supported by target (http/prompt/agent → command-only providers), updatedInput not supported (only CC/Gemini), async execution not supported, matcher syntax differences
- Could be part of the broader syllago compat command (syllago-u6b6) or standalone

CAPABILITY MATRIX TO ENCODE:
| Capability | CC | Gemini | Windsurf | Cursor | Cline | Copilot | Codex | Kiro |
| Block tool calls | Y | Y | Y | Y (3/6) | Y (PreToolUse) | Y (preToolUse) | N | Y (CLI) |
| Modify args | Y (updatedInput) | Y (rewrite) | N | N | Y (context inject) | Y | N | N |
| Handler types | cmd,http,prompt,agent | cmd | cmd | cmd | executable | cmd | cmd | askAgent+cmd |
| Async execution | Y | N | N | N | N | N | N | N |

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
- Installing a hook with 'type: http' to Windsurf produces a warning
